steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding damma at start and sukun at end. The middle weak letter و drops out in this form.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: I seek protection (response to قُلْ - say!)
steps.n: Changed to present tense first person by adding أَ at start and changing vowels. This word is what we are commanded to say by the word قُلْ (say!) that comes before it.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up
lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who intensely/completely does the action of nurturing and caring
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'

steps.m: by/with the Lord (object of أَعُوذُ - I seek refuge)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the seeking refuge happens. This word connects to أَعُوذُ (I seek refuge) in the ayah.

steps.m: people (those who are social/friendly by nature)
steps.n: Changed the concept of being social into a word for the beings who are social by nature (humans)
Added ال to specify we're talking about all people as a group, and the ن gets a shadda when ال is added

steps.m: of the people (object of أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word follows رَبِّ (Lord of) in the phrase 'Lord of the people'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
The kasra vowel shows this is about entering into a state of being a ruler
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word after it (ٱلنَّاسِ), making it mean 'king of the people'
The أ is dropped and ا is added after ن to make a word for people who have this quality of being social/familiar with each other

steps.m: the people (object of مَلِكِ - king of)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the beginning which causes the ن to double (because it's a sun letter). Added kasra at the end because this word is the object of مَلِكِ (king of).

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as deity

steps.n: Root letters formed into a noun meaning 'one worthy of worship'
The alif after second letter makes the 'aa' sound longer
Added ِ at the end to show this god belongs to or is associated with الناس (the people) that comes after it

steps.m: people (those who are familiar/social with each other)
steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be social' into noun meaning 'social beings/people'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is being possessed or follows a preposition
This is a basic preposition that shows something moving away from or originating from something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word شَرِّ (evil) to show what we're seeking protection from.
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it. It's like adding 'from' before a word in English.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger - showing something very evil
The root letters ش-ر-ر are squeezed together with a shadda (double letter), making the strong word for 'evil'
Added kasra to connect it to 'the whisperer' - showing it's describing how evil the whisperer is
Connected the root letters with vowels. This is a special pattern that repeats sounds to show something that keeps happening over and over.

steps.m: one who whispers evil thoughts (the evil whisperer)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'one who does this action'. The alif shows this is someone who does this action a lot.
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because this word belongs to شَرِّ (evil) in the phrase 'evil of the whisperer'

steps.m: one who repeatedly withdraws and sneaks back
steps.n: Added shadda (doubled ن) to show this happens repeatedly, and added vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: the one who repeatedly withdraws and sneaks back
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific - this refers to a particular sneaky withdrawer (Satan)

steps.m: of the one who repeatedly withdraws and sneaks back (describing الوسواس)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word describes الوسواس (the whisperer) in the phrase 'from the evil of the whisperer, the withdrawer'
This is the base word that connects a description to something being described, like saying 'the person who runs' or 'the book which is red'
Adding ٱلَّ at the beginning makes it point to something specific. In this ayah, it points to the one who does the whispering (يُوَسْوِسُ)
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks. The first وَ shows action starting, سْ shows quietness, وَ shows continuation, and سَ shows completion.
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at the start to show 'he' and ُ at the end to show it's happening now. This describes what الَّذِى (the one who) is doing.
This is the basic form of the preposition showing location inside something. Here it shows the whispering happens inside people's chests.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning.
The root becomes a noun meaning the physical chest/breast
Made plural by adding u-vowels inside the word, changing 'chest' to 'chests'
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition فِى (in). This shows it's connected to the word ٱلنَّاسِ (the people) to mean 'in the chests of the people'

steps.m: to be social, to feel comfortable with others
steps.n: Root relates to human companionship and society
The root أنس (being social) becomes نَّاس (people) by dropping the first letter and doubling the ن to emphasize many people together

steps.m: the people (who are whispered to in their chests)
steps.n:
1. Adding 'ٱل' at beginning makes it specific ('the people')
2. Adding kasra (ِ) at end shows this word follows 'صُدُورِ' (chests of)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word. This preposition introduces what types of whisperers are being talked about - those from among the jinn and mankind.

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight
steps.n: The root letters show the basic idea of something being hidden or concealed
The root meaning 'hidden' becomes a plural noun meaning 'hidden beings' by adding the vowels
The extra 'n' sound (shadda) makes it mean a specific group of jinn rather than just any hidden beings
Adds the definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: of the jinn (connected to مِنَ before it)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to مِنَ (from) before it, making the phrase mean 'from the jinn'

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
steps.n: The root relates to being social and friendly with others
Root أنس became نَّاس (people) with shadda for emphasis
Added 'the' at beginning and kasra at end to show it's connected to the word before it (مِنَ)
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to the previous phrase مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ (from the jinn)
